panels.title: Device Status
panels.description: Shows all 15 registered EcoFlow devices. Green = online and reporting data. Red = offline or disconnected. Includes smart plugs, PowerStream, and DELTA 2 Max.

panels.title: Daily Smart Plug Consumption
panels.description: Estimated daily energy based on the average power draw over the last 24 hours. Formula: average watts × 24h ÷ 1000 = kWh.

panels.title: Estimated Daily Cost
panels.description: Estimated daily cost based on 24h average power draw × electricity price. Only covers the 11 monitored smart plugs (~53% of total household).

panels.title: Smart Plug Energy Ranking
panels.description: Current power draw per plug, sorted highest first. This is the instantaneous value right now — not an average.

panels.title: Smart Plug Power Consumption
panels.description: Real-time power draw from all 11 smart plugs, stacked to show each plug's contribution. The dashed line shows the total. Hover over the chart to see individual values at any point in time.

panels.title: Smart Plug Cost Projection
panels.description: Estimated cost for the 11 monitored smart plugs only (~53% of total household). Based on the last 24h average power draw, extrapolated forward. Does NOT include unmonitored devices (lights, oven, heating, etc.).

panels.title: Smart Plug Energy Projection
panels.description: Estimated energy for the 11 monitored smart plugs only. Based on the last 24h average. For total household numbers, see the Household Electricity dashboard.

panels.title: Peak Power (24h)
panels.description: Highest power draw recorded in the last 24 hours per plug. Useful for spotting which appliances have the biggest spikes (e.g., dishwasher heating cycle, washing machine spin).

panels.title: Plug Temperature
panels.description: Internal temperature of each smart plug. Normal range is 20-40°C. Yellow zone (35°C+) means the plug is warm. Red zone (45°C+) could indicate overload.

panels.title: WiFi Signal Strength
panels.description: WiFi signal strength (RSSI) for each plug. Green (> -50 dBm) = strong. Yellow (-50 to -60) = fair. Orange (-60 to -70) = weak. Red (< -70 dBm) = poor, may cause data gaps.

panels.title: Device Uptime
panels.description: How long each plug has been running since its last reset, in days. A low number means the plug recently restarted (power outage, firmware update, or manual reset).

panels.title: Energy Share by Device
panels.description: Shows how total energy consumption is distributed across your smart plugs over the selected time range. The bigger the slice, the more energy that device uses relative to others.

panels.title: Top 5 Consumers Over Time
panels.description: Shows the 5 highest-consuming smart plugs over time. Useful for spotting which devices drive your electricity bill and when they're most active.
